title: Cache stampede
prompt_md: A single very popular cache key has a 60-second TTL. What is a **cache stampede** (thundering herd), and what is a valid fix?
options: When the key expires, many concurrent requests all miss at once and hammer the database with the same query simultaneously; fix it with locking/single-flight, early recompute, or jitter on the TTL
When the cache server runs out of memory and deletes every key; fix it by buying more RAM
When two users request different keys at the same time; fix it by adding more keys
When the TTL is set too long, so data never refreshes; fix it by removing the cache entirely
explanation_md: At the moment a hot key expires, every concurrent request sees a miss and runs the expensive recompute at the same time — because none has finished writing the value back yet. That synchronized spike can overwhelm the DB. Fixes: a **lock / single-flight** so only the first miss recomputes while others wait; **early/probabilistic recompute** so the value is refreshed before it expires; and **jitter** (randomized TTL offsets) so many keys don't all expire at the same instant.
